title: simmondsiaceae
intro: The Simmondsiaceae rank refers to a family of plants, which is a taxonomical rank used to classify and categorize plants based on their shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships. This family consists of flowering plants, including the jojoba shrub (Simmondsia chinensis), which is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The Simmondsiaceae family is relatively small, containing only one genus, Simmondsia, and a single species, S. chinensis. The family is known for its unique seed oil, which is used in cosmetics and pharmaceutical applications.
